summary: ANDROID ⛔ HSFWCH-9500 🟥 INTERMITTENT | TERN | EMU | OSPREY 🦅 Tapping on button on earbud during fit test will cause it to fail
scenario: Fit Test fails inconsistently when pressing earbud buttons on supported earbuds (Emu, Tern, Osprey)
description_clean: During a Fit Test on supported earbuds (Emu, Tern, Osprey), tapping a button on an earbud causes the fit test to fail or behave inconsistently. If the primary earbud button is pressed, the test shows a "device disconnected" failure message while the other earbud continues playing test audio. If the non-primary earbud button is pressed, the test lingers and eventually shows a message that the test cannot be completed at this time. Expected behavior: pressing a button on an earbud should not affect the fit test, or the app should handle it gracefully and consistently for both primary and non-primary earbuds.
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app on the Android device.
2. Navigate to Home screen and ensure supported earbuds (Emu, Tern, or Osprey) are connected.
3. Go to Device Settings → [Connected Earbuds Name] → Audio Settings.
4. Locate and start the Fit Test feature.
5. While the fit test is playing audio, press the button on the primary earbud.
8. While the fit test is playing audio, press the button on the non-primary earbud.
expected: Pressing a button on either the primary or non-primary earbud during a fit test should not affect the test. The fit test should continue normally and complete successfully without any error messages.
actual: When the primary earbud button is pressed during the fit test, the test shows a "device disconnected" failure message while the other earbud continues playing test audio. When the non-primary earbud button is pressed, the test lingers and eventually shows a message that the test cannot be completed at this time.
